result:
**Goal: Add a "Phase Space Navigator" mode that maps the behavioral landscape of cellular automaton rule-space as an interactive, real-time bifurcation explorer.**

The user sweeps continuously across two rule parameters (e.g., birth threshold vs. survival threshold, or density vs. neighbor radius) while the simulator runs hundreds of micro-simulations in parallel behind the scenes, plotting a live 2D heatmap of where complexity, chaos, oscillation, and death occur. Think of it as a "map of all possible universes" — the user can click any point on the phase portrait to instantly teleport into that simulation running full-screen.

**Why this is the strategic next step:** The last 5 commits have been building toward *understanding emergence* (Genesis Protocol explores rules, Butterfly Effect tracks causality, Computation Detector finds hidden structure). But all of these are local — they examine one simulation at a time. A phase space navigator gives a **global view**, showing the user the entire landscape of possible behaviors at once and revealing the narrow edge-of-chaos regions where interesting dynamics live. It's the natural culmination of the analytical trajectory this project is on.
